title: kalmia microphylla var. occidentalis
intro: Kalmia microphylla var. occidentalis, also known as the western mountain laurel, is a rare and endangered evergreen shrub native to the mountainous regions of western North America. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to treat various health issues such as skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Kalmia microphylla var. occidentalis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and are often used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
